titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.16

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=642 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:29:49.840055+00 | translation moa=643 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:29:53.141758+00

div.p:
W . dei gracia Rex Scot’. Omnibus probis Hominibus Totus terre sue Clericis et laicis Salutem . Sci
ant presentes et futuri me dedisse et concessisse et Hac carta mea confirmasse deo et Ecclesie
Sancte Marie de Melros . et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus unum plenarium Toftum
in Burgo meo de lanarc . Tenend’ in liberam et puram et perpetuam Elemosinam . ita libere
Quiete Plenarie et Honorifice  Sicut alias Elemosinas suas . liberius . Quietius . Plenius et Hono
rificentius  tenent . et possident . Test’ . Hug’ Cancellario . meo . Philippo de valoin’ Camerario . Ric’
de prebenda . Willelmo de Haia . Willelmo Giffard’ . Dunec’. de Laceles . Apud pert Primo die Iunii .

William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know me to have given, conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God, the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, one full toft in my burgh of Lanark, to be held in free, pure, and everlasting alms, as freely, quitly, fully, and honourably as they freely, quitly, fully and honourably hold and possess their other alms. As witness: Hugh my chancellor; Philip de Valognes the chamberlain; Richard de Prebenda; William de la Haye; William Gifford; Duncan de Lascelles. At Perth on the first day of June.
